articles.headline: CJI unveils special roster to clear Supreme Court's oldest cases
articles.summary: Chief Justice Surya Kant has constituted special benches to exclusively hear the Supreme Court's oldest civil and criminal cases, some pending for decades. The move aims to institutionalize the disposal of legacy litigation and reaffirm public confidence in the judiciary by ensuring timely closure for litigants who have waited years for justice and logical conclusion to their cases.

articles.headline: Man returning home from work mowed down in Delhi hit-and-run
articles.summary: A 45-year-old man, Uday Kumar, was killed after a speeding Scorpio struck him in West Delhi's Rajouri Garden while he was walking home from work. The 25-year-old driver fled the scene but was arrested hours later. Police have impounded the vehicle involved in the accident, and the investigation is ongoing to determine if the driver was under the influence.

articles.headline: Upstream rains raise Yamuna levels in Delhi after dry spell
articles.summary: The water level in the Yamuna river in Delhi rose sharply to 204.05m following heavy rainfall in the hills upstream. This marks the first significant increase in water levels after an extremely dry summer that had resulted in water supply disruptions. Authorities are monitoring the situation as levels approach the warning mark of 204.50m.

articles.headline: Litigants in Mathura, Gyanvapi, Sambhal reject SC mediation
articles.summary: Both Hindu and Muslim parties in major religious site disputes, including the Shahi Eidgah-Krishna Janmabhoomi and Gyanvapi mosque cases, have declined a Supreme Court-proposed mediation process. Litigants prefer to have their age-old legal contentions adjudicated on their legal merits in court rather than pursuing the voluntary 'Supreme Court Action for Mediation' SAMADHAN program.

articles.headline: 2 minors dead, 4 feared drowned in twin outer Delhi incidents
articles.summary: Two boys, aged 8 and 10, drowned in a rainwater-filled excavation pit in Mukhmelpur village, while four others are feared drowned in the Yamuna river in Alipur in separate incidents in North Delhi. Search operations are underway for the four missing individuals, who reportedly slipped into the river while playing. Police have initiated a probe into the safety lapses at the excavation site.

articles.headline: Flipkart delivery agent held for flashing in Bengaluru
articles.summary: A 22-year-old Flipkart delivery agent was arrested after a woman alleged he forced his way into her Bengaluru apartment and flashed her. The incident occurred when the agent delivered an order and requested to use the washroom. The company stated it has terminated the employee and is cooperating with the police investigation. The accused has been charged under relevant sections of the BNS.

articles.headline: Flash floods, landslides as monsoon rains batter hills
articles.summary: Monsoon rains triggered flash floods in Jammu & Kashmir, landslides in Uttarakhand, and thunderstorms in Himachal Pradesh. The IMD has issued alerts for continued heavy rain across northern India. Rescue operations are ongoing in several districts where roads have been blocked, and authorities have advised residents in low-lying areas to remain vigilant against rising river levels.

articles.headline: Vietnam arrests Captain; India moves to bring home victims' remains
articles.summary: Vietnamese police arrested the captain of a speedboat that capsized near Phu Quoc island, killing 15 Indian tourists. The captain faces charges of violating navigation laws. The Indian embassy in Hanoi is coordinating the repatriation of the bodies, 15 of which have reached Ho Chi Minh City. Efforts are underway to ensure the earliest possible transport of the remains to India.

articles.headline: DDA SOP: Action within 72 hours against illegal construction
articles.summary: The Delhi Development Authority has issued a new Standard Operating Procedure mandating demolition actions against unauthorized constructions within 72 hours of detection. The guidelines include the use of drones and geo-tagged documentation for surveillance. The move aims to streamline enforcement and eliminate delays in taking action against illegal structural additions and new buildings.

articles.headline: World Cup grows more inclusive, but old hierarchies hold
articles.summary: Data analysis of the expanded 48-team 2026 FIFA World Cup shows that while more diverse nations are qualifying, traditional European and South American powers continue to dominate the final stages. Despite larger representation from Asia and Africa, the quarter-final and semi-final spots remain largely occupied by historic footballing giants, suggesting a persistent gap in high-level competitiveness.

articles.headline: Hormuz crisis returns as Iran, US escalate strikes
articles.summary: Tensions in the Strait of Hormuz spiked as the US and Iran exchanged retaliatory military strikes. After an Iranian drone attack on a container ship, the US responded with strikes on 140 targets across Iran. Iran subsequently retaliated with attacks in Bahrain and Kuwait. The escalation has raised global concerns over energy security and the safety of commercial shipping in the vital corridor.

articles.headline: CDS may soon present theaterisation strategy to Defence Minister
articles.summary: Chief of Defence Staff General Anil Chauhan is set to present a final draft on the theaterisation of the Indian military to the Defence Ministry. The plan proposes creating four new integrated theater commands to replace the current service-specific units. The move seeks to enhance joint operational efficiency, though it awaits final approval from the Prime Minister-led Cabinet Committee on Security.

articles.headline: Centre condemns attack on vessel; 1 Indian crew missing
articles.summary: India has condemned an attack on the commercial vessel GFS Galaxy off the coast of Oman, which left one Indian crew member missing. The vessel, a Cyprus-flagged container ship, was reportedly targeted by Iranian forces. The Indian embassy is coordinating search efforts for the missing sailor. India called for immediate de-escalation and a diplomatic solution to ensure regional maritime stability.

articles.headline: Air quality in capital turns 'poor', worst in nearly 90 days
articles.summary: Delhi's air quality deteriorated sharply to the 'poor' category with an AQI of 281, the highest reading in nearly three months. Authorities attributed the sudden drop to transboundary winds carrying dust from Afghanistan, Iran, and Pakistan. The CAQM is monitoring the situation, as high PM10 concentrations continue to affect the NCR despite a previously clean monsoon spell.

articles.headline: Delhi Fire Service yet to empanel third party auditors
articles.summary: A month after notifying rules for mandatory third-party fire safety audits, the Delhi Fire Service has not yet empanelled the necessary auditors. The delay is attributed to architects failing to submit required technical details to the department. The audit system was designed to reduce the risk of fires in residential and commercial buildings by outsourcing inspections to certified professionals.

articles.headline: A crisis of trust in Ayodhya temple town
articles.summary: Allegations of financial irregularities and 'donation theft' in the Ram Temple project have triggered an SIT probe in Ayodhya. Investigations revealed breaches in security protocols at donation counting centers and the unauthorized possession of temple funds by several individuals. The row has sparked political debates and calls for greater transparency in the management of the temple's trust.

articles.headline: RSS expresses grief over Ram temple donation irregularities
articles.summary: The Rashtriya Swayamsevak Sangh (RSS) has expressed deep concern over reported irregularities in the counting of donations at the Ram Temple in Ayodhya. During a meeting in Belagavi, the RSS leadership called for a thorough investigation to restore devotee trust and ensure that any 'decisive turn' in the probe leads to accountability for those responsible for the alleged mismanagement of funds.

articles.headline: Absconding for 36 years, Kerala man held for ticket theft
articles.summary: A 71-year-old man from Kerala, who had been absconding since 1988, was arrested in Karnataka for stealing a bus ticket book from a KSRTC conductor. The accused had assumed a new identity and lived as a domestic worker in Mysore for decades. He was finally traced following a tip-off to the Thiruvananthapuram police, ending a 36-year-old legal hunt in the petty theft case.

articles.headline: Many flavours of bun masala at Srinivas Chats
articles.summary: The iconic Srinivas Chats in Bengaluru continues to draw crowds for its unique 'bun masala' and chat varieties. The 25-year-old establishment is celebrated for its spicy, sweet, and crunchy local street food, particularly its signature filling of grated carrot, onion, and groundnuts. The shop remains a staple for food enthusiasts in the city's Gandhi Bazaar area.

articles.headline: Karnataka Cabinet rejig likely; Hariprasad may get inducted
articles.summary: Speculation is rife in Karnataka regarding a cabinet reshuffle by CM Siddaramaiah. Senior leader BK Hariprasad is considered a frontrunner for induction to balance regional and caste equations within the Congress party. The move comes amid pressure to accommodate loyalists and improve administrative efficiency ahead of upcoming local body elections in the state.

articles.headline: Lokayukta seeks nod to prosecute Zameer Ahmed Khan
articles.summary: The Karnataka Lokayukta has approached Governor Thaawarchand Gehlot for permission to prosecute state minister BZ Zameer Ahmed Khan in a disproportionate assets case. The probe alleges that Khan accumulated wealth worth Rs 25 crore beyond his known sources of income. The Governor's decision is awaited, while Khan has dismissed the allegations as politically motivated.

articles.headline: BJP leaders urge state to drop Kambala from Dasara
articles.summary: BJP leaders in Karnataka have requested the state government to exclude Kambala, the traditional buffalo race, from the official Dasara festivities in Mysuru. Critics argue that the event is distinct to coastal Karnataka's culture and may not align with the traditional heritage of the Mysuru festival. The government is currently reviewing the proposal amid varying public opinions.

articles.headline: DK Shivakumar announces state honours for singer S. Janaki
articles.summary: Karnataka Deputy CM DK Shivakumar has announced that the funeral of legendary playback singer S. Janaki will be conducted with full state honours. Janaki, known as the 'Nightingale of the South,' passed away in Mysuru at the age of 88. The government also plans to establish a permanent memorial or museum to showcase her six-decade-long contribution to Indian film music across multiple languages.

articles.headline: Sofa culture: Political shifts and TVK in Tamil Nadu
articles.summary: Political analysis in Tamil Nadu highlights the rise of Actor Vijay's TVK party and its potential impact on the DMK-led alliance. The entry of new players is forcing established parties to rethink their strategies regarding seat-sharing and community representation. The 'sofa' metaphor reflects the comfort of ruling coalitions currently being challenged by emerging populist movements.

articles.headline: Final body recovered as Wayanad landslide toll reaches eight
articles.summary: Rescue teams retrieved the eighth and final missing body from a landslide site in Wayanad, Kerala. The victim was found 350 metres downstream from the initial impact zone after a week-long search operation hindered by debris. Authorities have now officially concluded the search and rescue phase, shifting focus to rehabilitation and investigating the environmental causes of the tragedy.

articles.headline: Telangana man suspected of killing 6 children in Pocso case
articles.summary: Telangana Police have formed 12 teams to apprehend a man accused of murdering six people, including his own wife and children, following a POCSO case filed against him. The suspect allegedly committed the crimes after being shamed by his family over the legal charges. A massive manhunt is underway across multiple districts, with police using CCTV footage and call records to track his movement.

articles.headline: Salem tension after Ambedkar statue protest leads to stone-pelting
articles.summary: Tension prevailed in Salem, Tamil Nadu, after a protest over the installation of an Ambedkar statue turned violent. Protesters and locals engaged in stone-pelting, prompting police to use water cannons to disperse the crowd. Several individuals were injured, and security has been beefed up in the area to prevent further communal flare-ups as authorities investigate the unauthorized installation.

articles.headline: 110kg ganja seized in Kerala; four arrested by Excise
articles.summary: In a major drug bust, the Kerala Excise department seized 110 kg of ganja in Angamaly and arrested four individuals. The contraband was being transported in a long-distance train from Andhra Pradesh to Palakkad for local distribution. This seizure is part of an ongoing state-wide anti-narcotics drive aimed at curbing the supply of synthetic and organic drugs in urban centers.

articles.headline: Meta responds to MeitY notice on child abuse content
articles.summary: Meta has submitted its response to the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) regarding the proliferation of child sexual abuse material (CSAM) on its platforms. The company claims it uses advanced AI and manual moderation to remove millions of violating pieces of content. MeitY is currently reviewing the technical details to ensure compliance with India's strict IT rules.

articles.headline: Kejriwal launches nationwide signature drive against arrest
articles.summary: The Aam Aadmi Party (AAP) has launched a massive signature campaign across India to protest the arrest of Delhi CM Arvind Kejriwal. Party workers are visiting households to collect signatures and distribute pamphlets alleging the misuse of central agencies for political vendetta. The campaign aims to mobilize public opinion ahead of upcoming state and general elections.

articles.headline: Isro successfully tests Gaganyaan crew module propulsion systems
articles.summary: The Indian Space Research Organisation (Isro) successfully conducted critical tests on the Gaganyaan crew module's propulsion and stabilization systems. The tests simulated atmospheric re-entry conditions to ensure the module remains stable and its uprighting systems function correctly upon sea splashdown. This marks a major milestone for India's first human spaceflight mission scheduled for 2025.

articles.headline: Amit Shah launches drive to plant 12.5 million trees
articles.summary: Union Home Minister Amit Shah launched a massive plantation drive in Ahmedabad under the 'Ek Ped Maa Ke Naam' campaign, with a target of planting 12.5 million trees in a single day. Shah emphasized the government's commitment to increasing India's green cover and combating climate change. Similar drives were conducted in Uttar Pradesh, aiming for a total of 350 million saplings this season.

articles.headline: Nine dead as Russia and Ukraine exchange missile strikes
articles.summary: Ongoing conflict between Russia and Ukraine resulted in nine fatalities as both sides launched heavy missile and drone strikes. Russian forces targeted infrastructure in Kyiv and Odessa, while Ukrainian strikes hit border regions in Russia. The international community continues to call for a ceasefire as civilian casualties rise and energy grids across Ukraine face significant damage.

articles.headline: Zelenskyy dismisses Ukraine's Prime Minister after one year
articles.summary: In a surprise political move, Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy has dismissed Prime Minister Denys Shmyhal after just over a year in office. The reshuffle is seen as an attempt to revitalize the government's economic and defense strategies amidst the prolonged war. A new candidate for the position is expected to be announced shortly following parliamentary consultations.

articles.headline: TCS announces top management reshuffle to boost growth
articles.summary: Tata Consultancy Services (TCS) has restructured its senior leadership and created new business units focused on high-growth sectors like cybersecurity and AI. The rejig includes the appointment of new heads for regional and industry-specific divisions. These changes are aimed at sharpening the firm's focus on emerging technologies and improving service delivery for global clients.

articles.headline: Jannik Sinner retains Wimbledon crown for fifth Slam title
articles.summary: Jannik Sinner secured his second consecutive Wimbledon title and fifth career Grand Slam after defeating Alexander Zverev in a four-set final. The 23-year-old Italian dominated the match with his powerful baseline play and composure. With this win, Sinner solidifies his position as the world number one and continues his dominant run in the current professional tennis season.

articles.headline: Bellingham's late brace sends England to World Cup semi-final
articles.summary: Jude Bellingham scored two late goals, including a dramatic injury-time winner, to help England defeat Norway 2-1 and advance to the FIFA World Cup semi-finals. England struggled for much of the match but Bellingham's individual brilliance secured the victory. They will now face Argentina in a highly anticipated semi-final clash to be held later this week.

articles.headline: Alvarez goal helps Argentina beat 10-man Switzerland
articles.summary: Julian Alvarez scored a stunning solo goal to lead Argentina to a 3-1 victory over Switzerland in the World Cup quarter-final. Switzerland was reduced to 10 men early in the second half, allowing Argentina to dominate possession. Lionel Messi provided two assists as the defending champions kept their hopes of a title defense alive, reaching the semi-finals for the second time in a row.

articles.headline: TCS backs sovereign AI, in talks with Indian developers
articles.summary: TCS Chief Executive K Krithivasan supports "sovereign AI" where nations build their own AI models, citing concerns over data privacy, control, and national security. TCS is in talks with Indian developers to collaborate on this. The company believes open-source foundational models and partnerships with academic institutions are crucial for India's AI future and talent development.

articles.headline: Hormuz closure threatens India's gas, crude supplies
articles.summary: Geopolitical tensions and recent attacks in the Red Sea and Gulf of Aden threaten oil and gas supplies through the Strait of Hormuz, a critical chokepoint. India imports significant crude and LNG via this route. Higher insurance premiums and potential disruptions could impact energy security and fuel prices, necessitating alternative routes and strategic reserves.

articles.headline: India wins 5 gold medals at Physics Olympiad
articles.summary: India secured five gold medals at the 54th International Physics Olympiad (IPhO) 2024 held in Iran, tying with China, South Korea, and the US as the top performers. This marks India's best-ever performance at the IPhO, underscoring the nation's scientific talent and educational prowess in physics.

articles.headline: SBIFund Management IPO creates 13 crorepatis, employees
articles.summary: SBI Mutual Fund's proposed IPO, with its employee share scheme, has created 13 crorepatis (₹10 million+) among its employees, including seven who will earn over ₹20 crore each. The IPO values the company at ₹2 lakh crore. Employees hold 2.76% of the company's equity, with 12.83% of their holdings from previous schemes, creating significant wealth.

articles.headline: Cancer cases generate up to 25% revenue for private hospitals
articles.summary: Oncology departments contribute significantly to private hospital revenues, generating 20-25%. Treatments for breast, lung, and prostate cancers are the highest revenue earners. Industry experts attribute this to advanced technologies, multidisciplinary care, and rising cancer prevalence. High treatment costs mean 50-70% of out-of-pocket expenses for patients, highlighting the financial burden of cancer care.

articles.headline: Inflation forecast driven by surging food costs
articles.summary: The government forecasts retail inflation to breach the 4% mark by June 2025, primarily driven by surging food prices. This indicates continued pressure on household budgets and challenges for the RBI's monetary policy in controlling inflation, especially with persistent supply-side issues in agricultural commodities.

articles.headline: Private investor interest in solar module firms on the rise
articles.summary: Investments in solar module manufacturing are increasing, driven by strong demand and government support like the PLI scheme. Over the last 18 months, 28 entities announced plans for 263 GW of capacity, with a potential investment of ₹1,00,000 crore. This surge reflects the sector's growth and India's ambition for energy self-sufficiency, reducing reliance on imports.

articles.headline: SEBI employees will need to liquidate all ‘non-permitted investment’
articles.summary: SEBI has amended its employee regulations, requiring staff to liquidate all "non-permitted investments" within three months of appointment. This includes various securities beyond their family members' immediate holdings. The move aims to prevent conflicts of interest and maintain regulatory integrity, ensuring SEBI officials act impartially and avoid financial implications.

articles.headline: Retail inflation likely breached 4% mark in June
articles.summary: Retail inflation for June is expected to exceed the RBI's 4% target, primarily driven by rising food prices, particularly vegetables. Economists estimate June CPI to be around 4.2-4.5%, up from 3.27% in May. This persistent food inflation poses challenges for monetary policy decisions and could impact household budgets.

articles.headline: NTPC Board approves ₹20,000 cr for Chhattisgarh's Lara project
articles.summary: NTPC's Board has approved an investment of ₹20,000 crore for Stage-II of the Lara Super Thermal Power Project in Chhattisgarh. The expansion, comprising two 800 MW units, aims to meet growing electricity demand and enhance the state's power infrastructure. This significant investment is subject to government approvals and will boost energy security.

articles.headline: Q-comm eliminating middlemen benefits horticulture farmers
articles.summary: Quick commerce platforms like Blinkit and Zepto are enhancing market access for horticulture farmers by reducing reliance on middlemen. This direct procurement model offers farmers better prices, fresher produce for consumers, and quicker sales, improving their income and reducing post-harvest losses, particularly for perishables like onions and tomatoes.

articles.headline: Oman coast hit by cyclone
articles.summary: Cyclone Dana struck the coast of Oman, causing severe flooding and landslides in the Al Wusta and Dhofar governorates. The Indian Coast Guard is monitoring the situation and has issued advisories to Indian vessels in the Arabian Sea, particularly fishing boats, urging caution and safety measures in the region.

articles.headline: National security versus trade
articles.summary: Geopolitical tensions are forcing nations to prioritize national security over economic gains from trade. The US-China rivalry and conflicts like Ukraine have led to calls for de-risking supply chains and reducing dependence on adversaries. This shift risks higher costs and reduced efficiency but is deemed necessary for strategic autonomy and resilience in a fragmented world.

articles.headline: Unbribed trade is now the new norm
articles.summary: The global trading landscape is shifting towards a "values-based" approach, where trust and transparency are prioritized over purely transactional relationships. This new norm, driven by geopolitical and supply chain vulnerabilities, requires countries to align trade with national interests and ethical considerations, moving away from purely economic bribes or conditionalities.

articles.headline: The Wall Street Journal on housing
articles.summary: A new book, "House of the Dragon," highlights how exclusionary zoning in wealthy US communities exacerbates housing shortages and inequality. The article discusses how restrictive policies prevent new housing construction, driving up costs and creating a "national inheritance problem" for younger generations, impacting affordability and social mobility.

articles.headline: It’s all about the defence
articles.summary: The article discusses how escalating global conflicts are forcing nations to rethink alliances and strengthen defense. The Ukraine war and Israel-Hamas conflict highlight the need for robust military capabilities and reliable supply chains, emphasizing self-reliance and strategic partnerships in a volatile world order to ensure national security.

articles.headline: Lobbying in Karnataka
articles.summary: A recent decision by the Karnataka Cabinet to repeal the Karnataka State Lobbying Act, 1973, has sparked concerns among transparency advocates. The Act, though dormant, provided a legal framework for registering lobbyists. Its repeal could reduce accountability and increase avenues for undue influence in policymaking, raising governance issues.

articles.headline: Understanding the rise of agentic commerce
articles.summary: Agentic commerce, where AI agents make purchasing decisions on behalf of consumers, is gaining traction. These AI-driven systems leverage data to predict needs and execute transactions, potentially saving time and optimizing choices. However, challenges include building trust, data privacy concerns, ethical implications, and market adoption for these autonomous shopping agents.

articles.headline: Governments lose from top of the world
articles.summary: This book review discusses "Made in Nepal" by Sujeev Shakya, which analyzes why Nepal's governments have failed to capitalize on its strategic location and potential. It explores historical decisions, political instability, and the struggle to balance competing national and international interests, hindering economic progress and fostering a culture of fatalism.

articles.headline: Sherwin Suwaria on "Thinking Your Way Through Life"
articles.summary: This article reviews "Thinking Your Way Through Life" by Sherwin Suwaria, which emphasizes the importance of mental models, deep questioning, and diverse experiences for effective decision-making. It suggests that individuals and organizations can navigate complexity by continuously learning and adapting their perspectives to solve problems effectively.

articles.headline: Underage new reads
articles.summary: This section reviews three new books: "Rather Than Nothing" by Suraj Nagappan, which explores the theory of everything; "Power and the Pulpit" by Bob Murray, focusing on a pastor's impact; and "Barnagar: The Forgotten City" by Paramita Roy, delving into the history of an ancient city. These offer diverse literary perspectives.

articles.headline: Radio's fight to get its voice heard
articles.summary: FM radio stations are leveraging live digital content and tier-2/3 market penetration to attract listeners and advertisers. Despite challenges from digital platforms, radio maintains relevance through hyper-local content and community engagement, with many stations achieving growth in listenership and ad revenue, especially beyond metro cities, adapting to evolving media consumption habits.

articles.headline: Canny dichotomies: The lion and the login screen
articles.summary: This article explores how brands balance traditional marketing (e.g., Coca-Cola's World Cup campaign) with digital engagement. It discusses the "lion and login screen" paradox, emphasizing the need for authentic messaging and emotional connection in both physical and digital realms to build brand loyalty effectively and reach diverse consumer segments.

articles.headline: Why behavioral lock-in is key to consumer business
articles.summary: Behavioral lock-in, where consumers repeatedly choose products due to habit or convenience, is crucial for business success. This article highlights how companies use incentives, subscription models, and personalized experiences to foster loyalty, making it harder for consumers to switch to competitors, even when better alternatives exist. This strategy builds enduring relationships.

articles.headline: Why the energy sector isn’t AI-ready yet
articles.summary: The energy sector faces challenges in AI implementation despite its potential benefits. Legacy systems, data silos, and a lack of standardized data prevent widespread AI adoption. Significant investment in infrastructure, training, and strategic partnerships are needed to realize AI's full potential in improving efficiency, operations, and grid management within the sector.

articles.headline: Geo-engineering against climate change
articles.summary: Geo-engineering techniques, such as stratospheric aerosol injection, are being explored as potential solutions to mitigate climate change. These methods aim to reduce global temperatures but pose significant risks, including unpredictable regional climate impacts and governance challenges, necessitating careful research and international cooperation before any large-scale deployment.

articles.headline: Global delivery hub: TCS to set up AI-led center
articles.summary: TCS is establishing a Global Delivery Hub focused on AI and digital transformation in Kolkata. This center will serve global clients, leverage AI capabilities, and employ over 20,000 professionals, reinforcing TCS's commitment to talent development and innovation in emerging technologies. It aims to accelerate adoption of AI-led transformation across industries.

articles.headline: ZincGel vs Li-ion battery
articles.summary: ZincGel batteries are emerging as a safer, more sustainable alternative to lithium-ion batteries. ZincGel offers advantages in cost, safety (non-flammable), and environmental impact, making it suitable for grid-scale energy storage and electric vehicles, addressing concerns associated with lithium's supply chain and fire risks. This technology could revolutionize energy storage.

articles.headline: IIT-M researches forgotten liquid to industrial wastewater treatment
articles.summary: IIT Madras researchers are developing new technology for industrial wastewater treatment using a 'forgotten' liquid called methyl tertiary-butyl ether (MTBE) and a bioreactor. This innovative method aims to efficiently remove pollutants, offering a cost-effective and environmentally friendly solution for industries like pharmaceuticals, ensuring cleaner water discharge and reduced environmental impact.

articles.headline: Cochin port bolsters gateway status
articles.summary: Cochin Port is strengthening its position as a major transshipment hub, with the Cochin International Container Transshipment Terminal (CICTT) playing a pivotal role. Investments in infrastructure, capacity expansion, and improved connectivity aim to attract more cargo and enhance India's maritime trade, reducing reliance on foreign ports and boosting economic growth.

articles.headline: Global shipping lines to call 'reckless attacks' on seafarers
articles.summary: Leading global shipping lines, including Maersk and Hapag-Lloyd, have condemned the increasing attacks on vessels in the Red Sea and Gulf of Aden as "reckless" and a threat to seafarers. The ongoing geopolitical tensions continue to disrupt global trade routes, forcing ships to take longer, more expensive detours and impacting supply chains.

articles.headline: DP World's growing track record for GCC
articles.summary: DP World's logistics operations are expanding significantly across the Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) region, with new investments in port infrastructure and supply chain solutions. The company aims to enhance regional trade connectivity and efficiency, leveraging its expertise in integrated logistics and port management, aligning with economic diversification strategies.

articles.headline: GAGAN: Navigating India's skies with precision
articles.summary: GAGAN (GPS Aided Geo Augmented Navigation) is enhancing air navigation in India, providing accurate and reliable positioning for civil aviation. This satellite-based system improves flight safety, efficiency, and allows for direct routing, reducing fuel consumption and environmental impact, vital for future air traffic growth and modernizing India's air space.

articles.headline: Aviation servicing on home soil
articles.summary: India is emerging as a major hub for aviation Maintenance, Repair, and Overhaul (MRO) services, driven by growth in air travel and government support. Companies like Air India Engineering Services and GMR are investing in MRO facilities, aiming to create jobs and reduce reliance on foreign MROs, making India self-reliant in aviation maintenance and boosting the economy.

articles.headline: Safe landings in Mangaluru
articles.summary: Mangaluru International Airport has implemented advanced lighting systems, including LED runway edge lights and a high-intensity approach lighting system. These upgrades, part of a ₹200 crore investment, enhance visibility for pilots, especially in adverse weather, ensuring safer and more efficient flight operations, aligning with international standards and improving airport capacity.

articles.headline: RE Posts: Powering India's Green Future
articles.summary: RE Posts highlights India's progress in renewable energy, aiming for 500 GW capacity by 2030. Initiatives like the Wind-Solar Hybrid Policy and the National Green Hydrogen Mission are driving investments and promoting clean energy, crucial for sustainable growth and energy security. This reflects India's commitment to climate goals.

articles.headline: Goal-oriented remedies
articles.summary: This article explores how Goal-Oriented Traditional Behavioral Therapy (GOT-CBT) is an effective mental health intervention, particularly when integrated with India's traditional wisdom. It helps individuals manage stress, anxiety, and depression by combining practical CBT techniques with cultural insights, promoting self-awareness and resilience for holistic well-being.

articles.headline: Spotlighting gender gap in pharma R&D
articles.summary: European women account for only a third of researchers in the pharmaceutical sector, highlighting a significant gender gap in R&D. The article advocates for policies promoting diversity and inclusion to foster innovation, suggesting that female scientists often bring diverse perspectives critical for drug discovery and development, impacting product safety and efficacy.

articles.headline: Reviving the cashew spread
articles.summary: India's cashew industry is undergoing revival efforts through initiatives like the Cashew Corpus Fund and technology upgrades. Historically, India was a major producer, but falling yields and imports led to decline. The aim is to boost domestic production and regain global market share, supporting farmer livelihoods and economic stability in the sector.

articles.headline: Data safety law must treat health records distinctly
articles.summary: The upcoming Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act) 2023 needs to treat health data distinctly due to its sensitive nature. Concerns exist over standardizing consent for health records, potentially compromising patient privacy and data security. A separate framework for health data could ensure better protection and responsible use, preventing misuse of sensitive information.

articles.headline: Generic semaglutide adoption: It's all about the delivery
articles.summary: The article discusses the potential adoption of generic semaglutide for diabetes and obesity, emphasizing the importance of diverse delivery methods beyond injectables. Innovations in oral or transdermal patches could enhance patient compliance and expand market access, making these treatments more accessible and effective for a wider population, addressing lifestyle diseases.

articles.headline: My five good habits
articles.summary: This brief article describes five daily habits for maintaining well-being, including an early morning routine, mindful eating, evening walks, reading, and spending quality time with family. It emphasizes consistency and simplicity for a balanced lifestyle, promoting overall physical and mental health through routine activities.

articles.headline: Concerns over restricted access to frontier AI models overblown
articles.summary: TCS CEO K Krithivasan believes concerns about restricted access to frontier AI models are "overblown." He advocates for democratizing AI and building sovereign AI capabilities within India to ensure data privacy, control, and national security, fostering an open-source ecosystem for innovation and talent development, rather than relying solely on global models.

articles.headline: New business verticals at TCS
articles.summary: TCS is reorganizing its business into new verticals, including Black Box AI, Data & Analytics, and Enterprise Contextual AI. This aims to better serve clients, leverage AI capabilities, and streamline operations. The new structure will focus on innovation and enhance customer engagement across various industries, adapting to evolving market demands.

articles.headline: Ethanol fuel push sparks consumer fury, Centre defends blending programme
articles.summary: The Centre defends its ethanol blending program, seeking to push beyond 20% amid consumer anxieties over potential vehicle damage and costs. A survey showed 66% reported over 10% mileage drop in pre-2023 cars. Auto industry requested E10 fuel availability alongside E20, citing Brazil's gradual transition, and sought a mandate delay to 2028. Government ruled out "protection fuel" due to management challenges and cited a 2021 roadmap for the April 2025 E20 rollout.

articles.headline: Several European countries meet Nato defence spending target early
articles.summary: Amid global conflicts, Nato members like Lithuania, Estonia, Latvia, and Poland are rapidly increasing defence spending, meeting the 3.5% of GDP target by 2035 ahead of schedule. Denmark is also close, while US spending fell below 3.2% in 2025.

articles.headline: Europe emerges as fastest-warming large habitable continent globally
articles.summary: Europe is rapidly warming, with Western Europe experiencing its hottest June on record (3.06°C above average), leading to thousands of deaths. It's now the fastest-warming continent after the Arctic, attributed to cleaner skies, shrinking snow cover, and proximity to the Arctic.

articles.headline: HBO tops Emmy nominations again in 2026
articles.summary: HBO led 2026 Emmy nominations with 122 nods ("The Pitt," "Hacks"), followed by Netflix (111) and Apple TV+ (89). While HBO topped nominations, 2025 results showed it tied with Netflix for wins, suggesting nominations don't guarantee awards dominance.

articles.headline: Indian Bank reports strong Q1 earnings, expands net interest margin
articles.summary: Indian Bank's Q1FY27 net profit rose 10% year-on-year to ₹3,273 crore, driven by strong treasury income. Gross NPAs declined to 1.86%, and net interest margin expanded to 3.29%, bucking the trend of margin compression seen by many banks. The bank prioritizes portfolio quality and profitability over aggressive growth.

articles.headline: TCS rejigs top deck, PE investors head back to school
articles.summary: TCS undertakes its largest leadership reshuffle in three years, moving a dozen top executives and reorganizing business units. The aim is to sharpen accountability and revive growth after the first annual revenue decline since listing in 2004, amidst high senior management churn. Meanwhile, PE investors accelerate return to physical schools, reversing a pandemic-era edtech trend, evaluating K-12 assets for strong operating performance and resilient cash flows.

articles.headline: Banks prioritize big depositors in FCNR race
articles.summary: Banks are prioritizing million-dollar-plus FCNR deposits from overseas Indians due to rising overseas borrowing costs and tighter credit limits. This shift differs from earlier optimism post-RBI's June 5 announcement for foreign currency inflows. Teething troubles, rising funding costs, and credit risk are factors, making smaller-ticket business less attractive.

articles.headline: Govt asks PSBs to fast-track JanSamarth loan disbursals
articles.summary: The finance ministry urged state-run banks to integrate the JanSamarth portal with lending systems and resolve reasons behind the 43.2% loan rejection rate in FY26. Incomplete documents and incorrect information are key issues. JanSamarth processed 13.1 million sanctioned loans in FY26, more than three times FY25's 4 million, expanding its scope to include CGTMSE and ECLGS 5.0.

articles.headline: Monsoon to be uneven, heavy rain in North-East
articles.summary: India expects an uneven monsoon, with heavy to very heavy rain forecast for the North-East, West Bengal, and Bihar over the next two to three days, while much of North-West, central, and peninsular India is expected to remain relatively dry. IMD issued a low to moderate flash flood risk for several districts in Assam and Meghalaya.

articles.headline: Grain stocks at 5-year high provide El Niño buffer
articles.summary: India's central wheat and rice stocks are at an 81.75 million tonnes (mt) five-year high, nearly four times the prescribed buffer norm. This provides a cushion against supply disruption and price pressures amid El Niño threats to the kharif crop. Wheat procurement strongly rebounded, and rice stocks remained broadly consistent.

articles.headline: Finished steel imports jump 49% in Q1 FY27
articles.summary: India remained a net importer of finished steel in Q1 FY27, with imports surging 49% year-on-year to 2.064 million tonnes. Strong demand from export-oriented manufacturers, particularly pipe makers for West Asia projects, drove imports despite safeguard duties. Private sector dominated crude and finished steel production, increasing 8% year-on-year.

articles.headline: Niche quick-commerce players prioritize quality over speed
articles.summary: Venture capital-backed quick-commerce startups in baby care, medicines, and fashion are prioritizing assortment, curation, strong supply chains, and healthier unit economics over ultra-fast delivery times. This shift reflects a more pragmatic phase in the sector after some rapid fashion startups struggled, betting that customers seek reliable availability and curated choices over 10-minute deliveries.

articles.headline: Indian Bank CEO targets $2 billion FCNR(B) deposits
articles.summary: State-owned Indian Bank aims to mobilize $2 billion in foreign currency deposits under RBI's special FCNR(B) window, having already secured $140 million. CEO Binod Kumar noted pricing, not demand, is the key challenge, urging banks to support RBI's objective of attracting dollar inflows even at a 'no-profit, no-loss' basis if required.

articles.headline: Banks Q1 preview: strong loan growth to offset margin squeeze
articles.summary: Indian banks are expected to report strong loan growth but capped profit growth in Q1FY27 due to lower treasury income, margin pressure, and higher provisioning. Private banks may see 11% earnings growth, while PSUs decline 2% due to a one-off provision. Deposit growth lags lending, intensifying competition for liabilities.

articles.headline: Jindal Steel hit by fresh leadership churn as CEO exits
articles.summary: Jindal Steel Ltd's CEO Gautam Malhotra is exiting after just nine months, continuing a pattern of senior management churn. Rajiv Kumar is expected to succeed him. The four-day gap between resignation announcement and last day raises governance concerns for the company, which has seen six CFOs since 2020.

articles.headline: Aera moves to shield flyers from advance project costs
articles.summary: India's airport tariff regulator (Aera) proposes that airports recover major expansion project costs only after assets are operational, not upfront. This would shift funding burden from passengers to operators during construction and aims to discourage project delays. The proposal was prompted by large investment plans from Bengaluru and Hyderabad airports.

articles.headline: HDFC Bank CEO: Chairman exit a challenging event
articles.summary: HDFC Bank CEO Sashidhar Jagdishan acknowledged former chairman Atanu Chakraborty's resignation in March as a "challenging event" that raised corporate governance questions. An independent legal review, involving US and Indian law firms, found no basis for Chakraborty's unspecified claims. Rajiv Kumar was appointed part-time chairman.

articles.headline: Dawaa Dost eyes first equity raise of ₹250 crore
articles.summary: Omni-channel generic medicine pharmacy network Dawaa Dost plans to raise ₹200-250 crore in its first institutional equity funding round, appointing EY for the process. Funds will expand its reach and omnichannel network. Founded in 2018, it serves 19,000 pincodes with 80+ offline pharmacies, leveraging a proprietary Kirana Tech model.

articles.headline: Hero flags CAFE norms as risk to two-wheeler firms
articles.summary: Hero MotoCorp, India’s largest two-wheeler maker, identified proposed corporate average fuel efficiency (CAFE) norms from April 2028 as an "emerging risk," warning tougher fuel-efficiency rules could raise costs in a price-sensitive market. The company plans mitigation through lightweight components, vehicle efficiency, and expanding its EV portfolio.

articles.headline: H&M India ex-execs bet on premium fashion with Confluxe
articles.summary: Former H&M India executives Louis Coucke and Rajesh Narkar launched Confluxe to bring global premium apparel labels to India, targeting ₹500-1,000 crore business in 3-5 years. The asset-light model aims to fill the gap between fast fashion and luxury. Their first brand, Urban Performance, launched last week, with products priced between ₹3,999 and ₹20,000.

articles.headline: India’s PhD-holding entrepreneurs on the rise in deeptech
articles.summary: India's deeptech sector is seeing a rise in PhD entrepreneurs, with universities redesigning programs to encourage commercialization of research. This fills a talent gap in AI, semiconductors, and clean energy, addressing India's need for self-reliance in advanced technologies. Deeptech funding increased from $484 million in 2020 to $1.47 billion in 2025, with startups growing from 2,700 to over 4,200.

articles.headline: How to report equity and debt fund gains in your ITR
articles.summary: Taxpayers filing ITR for AY26-27 face detailed reporting for capital gains from shares and mutual funds, especially after recent tax changes. Debt fund gains classification depends on purchase date (before/after April 1, 2023). Manual reporting of scrip-wise details and quarterly break-ups is crucial to avoid disputes and incorrect interest computation. Capital losses should also be reported for carry-forward benefits.

articles.headline: Choosing the right ITR form for this year
articles.summary: Selecting the correct Income Tax Return (ITR) form is essential for accurate tax filing. The tax department introduced changes for AY26-27, including expanded eligibility for ITR-1 and ITR-4, and additional disclosures for donations and trading. Reviewing conditions carefully is advised to avoid delays or revised filings.

articles.headline: Reporting errors, not evasion, spark tax litigation
articles.summary: Many tax disputes arise from reporting mismatches between taxpayer ITRs and information from the tax department's Annual Information Statement (AIS), rather than deliberate evasion. Experts suggest reconciling AIS with supporting documents before filing and advocate for policy changes like pre-assessment reconciliation and statutory timelines for rectifications to reduce litigation.

articles.headline: Why AIS shouldn’t be the sole source for ITR filing
articles.summary: The Annual Information Statement (AIS) is a starting point for ITR filing but is not error-free or complete. Taxpayers must reconcile every AIS entry with supporting documents and report discrepancies via feedback. Common errors include duplicate entries, incorrect capital gains, and missing income data for SGBs, SSY, or cumulative FDs.

articles.headline: Funds fret over AI trio’s grip on emerging markets
articles.summary: Investors are diversifying beyond three dominant AI technology stocks (TSMC, Samsung, SK Hynix) that comprise over 30% of the MSCI Emerging Markets Index. Concerns over concentration and frothy valuations are prompting rotation into gaming, energy, and other sectors, especially in India and China, to safeguard clients' capital from over-concentration.

articles.headline: U.S. and Iran launch new attacks in fight for Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: US and Iran escalated fighting over the Strait of Hormuz, with the US hitting 140 Iranian targets with precision munitions and Iran attacking commercial vessels and threatening allies. This ends a recent ceasefire, threatening stability in a vital oil shipping route. A Cyprus-flagged containership was damaged by fire, with one civilian crew member missing.

articles.headline: Iran ready to escalate over Hormuz, aspiring to regional domination
articles.summary: Iran views its control over the Strait of Hormuz as key to its regional hegemony, believing concessions are won through coercion. Tehran's actions reflect its ambition to establish a new Pax Iranica in the Middle East, challenging US influence and risking further violence and global energy market uncertainty, despite its military and economy being degraded.

articles.headline: Airtel bets big on data centres and cloud for growth
articles.summary: Bharti Airtel, after investing over ₹3.3 trillion in digital infrastructure, plans its next growth phase in financial services, data centres, and cloud. Chairman Sunil Mittal highlighted a ₹20,000 crore capitalization for Airtel Money and Nxtra's $1 billion raise for 1 GW data centre capacity, underscoring conviction in these adjacencies.

articles.headline: Trai wants Meta, Google to share spam reports with telcos
articles.summary: Trai is in talks with Meta Platforms Inc. and Google Llc to integrate spam complaints from platforms like WhatsApp and in-built phone diallers with telecom operators' complaint systems. The aim is to identify repeat offenders and act faster against spammers, though Meta has expressed hesitation due to user privacy and technical issues.

articles.headline: EPFO calls PF trusts to regularize status with amnesty scheme
articles.summary: The Employees’ Provident Fund Organisation (EPFO) has launched an amnesty scheme for provident fund trusts to regularize their status over six months. This initiative aligns with the Finance Act, 2026, allowing trusts recognized under the Income Tax Act, 1961, to obtain exemption under the EPF Act, 1952.

articles.headline: SAIL, Indonesia’s Krakatau in JV pact for stainless steel
articles.summary: Steel Authority of India Ltd (SAIL) and Indonesia's PT Krakatau Steel are exploring a joint venture to set up a stainless steel project of 500,000-1 million tonnes capacity. Slabs would be produced in Indonesia and supplied to SAIL’s Salem Steel Plant, catering to increasing demand in India.

articles.headline: India says 10 nationals saved off Oman, 1 missing
articles.summary: India reported 10 of its nationals rescued, and one missing, from a commercial vessel, GFS Galaxy, struck off Oman. US Central Command attributed the attack, which disabled the ship by fire and damaged its engine room, to Tehran amid escalating US-Iran tensions in the Strait of Hormuz. Search and rescue operations are ongoing.

articles.headline: Swiggy Instamart gets FSSAI notices over food complaints
articles.summary: FSSAI issued nine notices to Swiggy Ltd’s quick commerce unit Instamart following consumer complaints alleging supply of expired, spoiled, and contaminated food products. Issues include unfit eggs, incorrect FSSAI license numbers, and inadequate grievance redressal. The regulator has stepped up enforcement in recent years.

articles.headline: AI job fears give way to optimism, say business leaders
articles.summary: Business leaders, including OpenAI CEO Sam Altman, are shifting from warnings of mass AI-driven job losses to an optimistic view of AI boosting productivity and creating new jobs. Studies suggest companies investing in AI are also growing employment, despite earlier public sentiment and some tech layoffs. Ford, for example, hired engineers due to AI-driven quality gains.

articles.headline: Incentives for Keeping More BPO/IT Staff at Home
articles.summary: The government plans to extend work-from-home incentives for BPO/IT companies beyond July 31. New rules will allow remote working for up to 50% of staff, requiring registration and network security compliance to support the industry.

articles.headline: New Power Policy May Reach Cabinet in August
articles.summary: A draft national power policy is ready for Cabinet consideration by August. It proposes a new framework for generation, distribution, and transmission, focusing on clean energy, smart grids, and addressing discom financial health for sustainable development.

articles.headline: TCS Sees Better Q2 Show on Stressed Sectors' Turnaround
articles.summary: TCS CEO Rajesh Gopinathan expects an improved Q2 performance, driven by a turnaround in previously stressed sectors like manufacturing, energy, and travel. The company focuses on cloud, data analytics, and cybersecurity services, anticipating stable demand.

articles.headline: New Delhi Condemns Taliban Violence; 11 Indians on Board
articles.summary: New Delhi condemned increased Taliban violence in Afghanistan, urging an immediate ceasefire. Eleven Indians were among 21 taken hostage from a civilian aircraft but have since been released, highlighting regional security concerns and India's involvement.

articles.headline: Pure Politics: ED Seeks Info on Funding Probe into Cyberfraud
articles.summary: The Enforcement Directorate (ED) seeks information from agencies probing an online multi-state cyberfraud network, suspecting political funding via shell companies. The agency has already attached properties worth ₹17 crore as part of its ongoing investigation.

articles.headline: Calcutta Stock Exchange Revival Plan Hits Snags
articles.summary: A revival plan for the Calcutta Stock Exchange (CSE) faces hurdles, including a requirement to become a full-fledged exchange. The CSE needs ₹100 crore in share capital and a revised business model to restart operations effectively.

articles.headline: Carlisle Consortium Set to Take Control of Defence Parts Supplier MicroPack
articles.summary: A consortium led by Carlisle is set to acquire a 90% stake in MicroPack, an Indian defence parts supplier, for $125 million. This deal involves a 60% stake from Carlyle and 30% from its existing PE fund.

articles.headline: N-Sub Refuelling Done, Kalpakkam U Reactor Pact to Be Signed
articles.summary: The refuelling of India's indigenous fast breeder reactor at Kalpakkam is complete. A pact for the supply of enriched uranium for nuclear power reactors will be signed between India and Russia, bolstering India's nuclear energy program.

articles.headline: Quadangular Security Setup To Be Operationalised by Dec
articles.summary: The Quadrangular Security Coordination Mechanism, covering 119 border districts across four states (UP, MP, Chhattisgarh, Rajasthan), will be operationalized by December. It aims to improve inter-state crime prevention and intelligence sharing.

articles.headline: How India, Australia Clinched Uranium Deal
articles.summary: India and Australia have finalized an agreement for uranium supply to India, following IAEA safeguards being accepted. This deal strengthens bilateral ties and provides India with a crucial energy source from Australian companies.

articles.headline: India Condemns Attack on Ship in Gulf of Oman
articles.summary: India condemned a missile attack on a commercial ship in the Gulf of Oman, emphasizing the importance of freedom of navigation. Four Indian nationals were aboard the Liberian-flagged vessel, which sustained minor damage from the incident.

articles.headline: UP Police Arrests Village Head Among Accused in Priest Killing
articles.summary: UP Police arrested a village head and others in connection with the murder of a priest, allegedly stemming from a land dispute. The priest was found dead in a temple, with the village head's son also implicated in the crime.

articles.headline: Distinctive Envelope of State Government
articles.summary: B Prasad comments on the unique characteristics and functioning of state governments, highlighting their distinct operational frameworks and policy approaches. This analysis delves into the nuances of governance at the sub-national level.

articles.headline: Army Eye One-Way Desi Drones With 1,000-km Strike Principle
articles.summary: The Indian Army plans to acquire long-range indigenous loitering munitions with a 1,000 km strike range and 50 kg warhead. These 'suicide drones' are designed for precision strikes and surveillance, enhancing offensive capabilities for the military.

articles.headline: Tamil Nadu Min Gifts Gold Rings To Newborns
articles.summary: Tamil Nadu minister M. Manikandan gifted gold rings to newborns at Ramanathapuram government hospital to mark CM Palaniswami's birthday. Similar initiatives have been undertaken by ministers in the past as gestures of goodwill.

articles.headline: U'khand Asks Badri Nath to Do Act Allowance from Officers
articles.summary: Uttarakhand has instructed the Badrinath-Kedarnath Temple Committee to recover allowances taken by officers/staff under the 2013 Act from 2014 to 2016. This follows an audit report citing irregularities in the disbursement of funds.

articles.headline: Ram Temple Trust To Decide CEO Powers At Overcome Law
articles.summary: The Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Teerth Kshetra Trust will clarify the CEO's powers, following objections from a member. The Trust plans to set clear rules for its functioning and decision-making regarding the Ayodhya temple construction.

articles.headline: Shri Inaugurates 'Mission 5 Million' Initiative in Ahmedabad
articles.summary: Union Home Minister Amit Shah inaugurated the 'Mission 5 Million' initiative in Ahmedabad, aiming to provide jobs to 5 million people over three years. He emphasized skill development and industrial growth to boost employment opportunities.

articles.headline: Inappropriate Content': 3 Publishers Arrested Over Controversial Books
articles.summary: Three publishers were arrested for printing and distributing books with 'inappropriate content,' including objectionable references to national heroes. This follows a police complaint and investigation into the content, sparking debate on censorship and freedom of expression.

articles.headline: ED Sends Letters Rogatory Abroad Seeking Info on Foreign Players
articles.summary: The Enforcement Directorate (ED) has sent Letters Rogatory to foreign countries, seeking details on individuals and entities involved in a multi-crore cyber fraud network. The agency suspects money laundering and illicit foreign transactions.

articles.headline: Legendary Playback Singer P Susheela's Son Passes Away
articles.summary: K.P. Jayakrishna, son of legendary playback singer P Susheela, passed away at 52 due to a heart attack in Chennai. He was a film producer and director, known for his work in the Telugu industry.

articles.headline: NIA Arrests 2 Accused in Pulwama Terror Attack Case
